record.title: Louis Fantin | Interview 1 | November 12, 2002

Community and Identity

point.title: Introduction / Italian immigrant family background / Childhood memories of growing up, racial dynamic and stigma in Sopris,Colorado

His migration to Richmond, California for economic opportunities / working at the Ford Richmond Plant, Pullman shop and the shipyard/ joining the Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO)/ Women's role and racial dynamic at the shipyard
His father's loyalty for Italy / Being exposed to racial and cultural diversity in California / Food and gasoline rationing /
Socializing, entertainment and dating life while working at the shipyard / Overcrowding and housing scarce in the city/ Disrespect towards women in the crew / Racism in the army
Disrespect towards women in the crew / Racism in the army

Being drafted, "Uncle Sam, 'We Want You.'" and his feelings about it / His memories being trained in England and breaking through in the peninsula of Saint- Lô and liberating Normandy / His attempts to visit Italy
